sources.title: strix
sources.description: Open-source AI-powered penetration testing agent that autonomously finds and fixes application vulnerabilities.

sources.title: page-agent
sources.description: In-browser GUI agent from Alibaba that controls web interfaces via natural language.

sources.title: chrome-devtools-mcp
sources.description: Official Chrome DevTools MCP server letting coding agents control and debug the browser directly.

sources.title: herdr
sources.description: High-performance Rust-based terminal multiplexer for running multiple AI agents at once.

sources.title: unity-mcp
sources.description: Bridges AI assistants with the Unity Editor to automate scene control and asset management.

sources.title: terax-ai
sources.description: A 7MB terminal-first local AI development workspace.

sources.title: caveman
sources.description: Claude Code skill that reduces token usage by up to 65% via compressed, cavemen-style context.

sources.title: codex-plugin-cc
sources.description: Plugin that runs OpenAI Codex from within Claude Code, bridging two agent systems for delegation and review.

sources.title: ECC
sources.description: Agent performance optimization system for Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor providing skills, instincts, and memory management.

sources.title: video-use
sources.description: Automates video editing workflows using browser-use-powered coding agents.

sources.title: career-ops
sources.description: Go-based AI-powered job search system built on Claude Code with a dashboard and 14 skill modes.

sources.title: agency-agents
sources.description: End-to-end AI agency framework composed of specialized expert agents (frontend, community management, etc.).

sources.title: superpowers
sources.description: Agentic skill framework and software development methodology.

sources.title: OmniRoute
sources.description: Free AI gateway for coding assistants routing 200+ LLM providers through one endpoint, with token compression and MCP/A2A support.

sources.title: CubeSandbox
sources.description: Tencent Cloud's open-source sandbox for running AI agents in concurrent, secure, isolated environments.

sources.title: agents-cli
sources.description: Official Google CLI and skill pack specializing any coding assistant in building, evaluating, and deploying AI agents on Google Cloud.

sources.title: ai-berkshire
sources.description: Value-investing research framework for Claude Code and Codex combining multiple investor methodologies via a multi-agent architecture.

sources.title: Agent-Reach
sources.description: Capability layer giving AI agents zero-fee read/search access to Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, and more.

sources.title: design.md
sources.description: Google Labs format for conveying design systems and visual identity to coding agents via combined YAML/Markdown files.

sources.title: agent-toolkit-for-aws
sources.description: Officially supported MCP servers and plugin pack for AI coding agents to interact with AWS services.

sources.title: MinerU
sources.description: Document parsing engine converting PDF/DOCX/PPTX into Markdown/JSON for RAG and agent workflows, with VLM/OCR support.

sources.title: hiring-agent
sources.description: AI agent that autonomously reads, evaluates, and scores resumes for hiring workflows.

sources.title: harness
sources.description: Meta-skill plugin for Claude Code that designs domain-specific agent teams by architecture pattern and generates the skills they use.

sources.title: ai-website-cloner-template
sources.description: Template that uses AI coding agents to analyze and clone a website's design into a modern Next.js/Tailwind project.

sources.title: container
sources.description: Apple's official open-source tool for creating and running Linux containers as lightweight VMs on macOS, optimized for Apple Silicon.

sources.title: openpencil
sources.description: Open-source AI vector design tool turning natural-language prompts into UI designs, with code output for React, Tailwind, Flutter, and SwiftUI.

sources.title: Kenney
sources.description: Free game asset packs (sprites, 3D models, UI kits, audio) and starter tools for game developers.

sources.title: awesome-multi-agent-papers
sources.description: A curated list of notable research papers on multi-agent LLM systems, maintained by kyegomez.

sources.title: Magma_Landing-Page_Clone
sources.description: GSAP, ScrollTrigger, Locomotive Scroll, and HTML Canvas clone with scroll-driven canvas frame changes, zoom, and scene transitions.

sources.title: gentlerain.ai
sources.description: Clone of the Gentlerain.ai Awwwards site built with GSAP, Three.js, and Lenis, showing WebGL and GSAP used together.

sources.title: JeskoJets-ScrollAnimation
sources.description: Scroll animation that zooms into the hero area instead of sliding past it, for perspective and 3D transition effects.

sources.title: Truus.co-Awwward-Website
sources.description: Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS site built with GSAP, ScrollTrigger, and Lenis featuring experimental layout, cursor interaction, and dense motion design.

sources.title: 3D-Landing-page-for-Apple-iPhone
sources.description: 3D iPhone product landing page using Three.js for rendering and GSAP/ScrollTrigger for scroll-controlled camera and product animation.

sources.title: capsule
sources.description: React, Vite, Tailwind, and GSAP recreation of the Awwwards-winning Capsule site's complex animations.

sources.title: SpyltMilk-clone
sources.description: Single-product Awwwards clone with bold, colorful motion design built with React, Tailwind, and GSAP.

sources.title: svelte-gsap-template
sources.description: Production-oriented, MIT-licensed creative agency landing page template built with Svelte 5, GSAP 3, and Tailwind CSS 4, featuring spatial typography and hardware-accelerated motion.

sources.title: cocktails-gsap
sources.description: Scroll-driven landing page built with React, Tailwind, and GSAP, with a clean folder structure for studying advanced GSAP animations.

sources.title: threejs-gsap-scroller
sources.description: Isolated example binding a Three.js 3D object to a ScrollTrigger timeline for scroll-controlled camera movement and 3D scenes.

sources.title: lenserfight
sources.description: Platform for parameterized prompts, AI-agent workflows, a community forum, and prompt battles.

sources.title: n8n
sources.description: Fair-code workflow automation platform with native AI capabilities, 400+ integrations, and self-hosting.

sources.title: hermes-agent
sources.description: Extensible AI agent with persistent memory designed to adapt to its user over time.

sources.title: gemini-cli
sources.description: An open-source AI agent that integrates Google Gemini capabilities into the command-line interface.

sources.title: hoppscotch
sources.description: Hoppscotch is an open-source API development ecosystem providing tools for testing and developing APIs across web, desktop, and CLI environments. It serves as an alternative to proprietary API clients like Postman and Insomnia.

sources.title: tensorflow
sources.description: TensorFlow is an open-source machine learning framework designed for everyone, supporting deep learning and neural networks. It provides tools for building and deploying machine learning models, including capabilities for distributed computing.

sources.title: codec
sources.description: An open-source intelligent command layer and framework for building and running local AI agents, supporting voice interaction and self-hosted deployments.

sources.title: Blockchain
sources.description: A growing list of records, called blocks, that are linked using cryptography.

sources.title: Artificial Intelligence
sources.description: Overview of intelligence demonstrated by machines, in contrast to natural intelligence displayed by humans and animals.

sources.title: Machine Learning
sources.description: The scientific study of algorithms and statistical models that computer systems use to perform tasks without explicit instructions.

sources.title: Decentralizing AI: Dreamers vs. Pragmatists
sources.description: Jesus Rodriguez, May 23, 2019 — commentary on the practical challenges of decentralizing AI.

sources.title: How the Blockchain Could Break Big Tech's Hold on A.I.
sources.description: New York Times, October 20, 2018 — how blockchain-based data/model sharing could challenge Big Tech's AI dominance.

sources.title: How to Actually Combine AI and Blockchain in One Platform
sources.description: Hacker Noon, June 7, 2018 — practical approaches to combining AI and blockchain in a single platform.

sources.title: Blockchain-based Machine Learning Marketplaces
sources.description: Fred Ehrsam, March 13, 2018 — on marketplaces for machine learning models built on blockchain.

sources.title: The convergence of AI and Blockchain: what's the deal?
sources.description: Francesco Corea, December 1, 2017 — an early look at where AI and blockchain intersect.

sources.title: SingularityNET
sources.description: A distributed AI platform on the Ethereum blockchain, with each blockchain node backing up an AI algorithm.

sources.title: Intuition Fabric
sources.description: Aims to democratize access to AI through a network of deep learning models stored on IPFS and accessed through the Ethereum blockchain.

sources.title: OpenMined
sources.description: A community building open-source technology for the decentralized ownership of data and intelligence, enabling AI to train on data it never accesses directly.

sources.title: Raven Protocol
sources.description: A decentralized and distributed deep-learning training protocol.

sources.title: Thought Network
sources.description: A blockchain-enabled fabric that embeds artificial intelligence into data, making it agile, actionable, and inherently secure.

sources.title: MATRIX AI
sources.description: A public chain combining AI technology with blockchain to solve challenges stifling blockchain adoption, using a blockchain-powered decentralized computing platform.

sources.title: Cortex Labs
sources.description: A decentralized AI platform with a virtual machine that allows executing AI programs on-chain.

sources.title: Fetch.ai
sources.description: A decentralized machine learning platform based on a distributed ledger, enabling secure sharing, connection, and transactions based on data globally.

sources.title: Oraichain
sources.description: An intelligent and secure solution for emerging Web3, scalable dApps, and decentralized AI.

sources.title: Bittensor
sources.description: An open-source protocol powering a decentralized, blockchain-based machine learning network.

sources.title: Alethea AI
sources.description: A research and development studio building at the intersection of Generative AI and Blockchain.

sources.title: Vanna Labs
sources.description: An Ethereum L2 rollup that supports native, seamless, and trustless AI/ML inferences on-chain to empower decentralized applications.

sources.title: Danku
sources.description: A blockchain-based protocol for evaluating and purchasing ML models on a public blockchain such as Ethereum.

sources.title: Decentralized & Collaborative AI on Blockchain
sources.description: A framework to host and train publicly available machine learning models in smart contracts with incentive mechanisms encouraging good-quality training data.

sources.title: Ocean Protocol
sources.description: A decentralized data exchange protocol that lets people share and monetize data while guaranteeing control, auditability, transparency, and compliance.

sources.title: TrueBit
sources.description: Gives Ethereum smart contracts a computational boost.

sources.title: DeepBrain Chain
sources.description: A decentralized AI computing platform that supplies processing power to companies developing AI technologies.

sources.title: Nunet
sources.description: A globally decentralized computing framework combining latent computing power of independently owned devices into a dynamic marketplace of compute resources.

sources.title: Phala Network
sources.description: A decentralized off-chain compute infrastructure for Web3 development.

sources.title: Numerai
sources.description: A hedge fund powered by a network of anonymous data scientists who build machine learning models on encrypted data and stake cryptocurrency to express confidence in their models.

sources.title: Cindicator
sources.description: A crowd-sourced prediction engine for financial and crypto indicators.

sources.title: Erasure
sources.description: A decentralized protocol and data marketplace for financial predictions.

sources.title: doc.ai
sources.description: Aims to decentralize precision medicine on the blockchain using AI.

sources.title: BurstIQ
sources.description: A healthcare data marketplace with granular ownership and consent of data; on-chain storage on a custom blockchain supports HIPAA, GDPR, and other regulations.

sources.title: Hashgraph Online (HOL)
sources.description: Universal agentic registry built on Hedera Hashgraph, providing blockchain-based identity for AI agents via the ERC-8004 standard and HCS-14 Universal Agent IDs, enabling discovery, verification, and autonomous commerce via x402.

sources.title: AgentFund
sources.description: A decentralized crowdfunding infrastructure for autonomous AI agents on Base blockchain, enabling milestone-based escrow funding for AI projects and collaborations.

sources.title: Coin.AI: A Proof-of-Useful-Work Scheme for Blockchain-based Distributed Deep Learning
sources.description: Baldominos, A., & Saez, Y. (2019). Entropy, 21(8), 723.

sources.title: WekaCoin: Proof-of-learning, a Blockchain Consensus Mechanism Based on Machine Learning Competitions
sources.description: Bravo-Marquez, F., Reeves, S., & Ugarte, M. (2019). IEEE DAPPCON 2019, pp. 119-124.

sources.title: DLBC: A Deep Learning-Based Consensus in Blockchains for Deep Learning Services
sources.description: Li, B., Chenli, C., Xu, X., Shi, Y., & Jung, T. (2019). arXiv:1904.07349.

sources.title: Energy-recycling Blockchain with Proof-of-Deep-Learning
sources.description: Chenli, C., Li, B., Shi, Y., & Jung, T. (2019). IEEE ICBC 2019, pp. 19-23.

sources.title: BlockML: A Useful Proof of Work System Based on Machine Learning Tasks
sources.description: Merlina, A. (2019). Proceedings of the 20th International Middleware Conference Doctoral Symposium, pp. 6-8.

sources.title: On the Convergence of Artificial Intelligence and Distributed Ledger Technology: A Scoping Review and Future Research Agenda
sources.description: Pandl, K. D., Thiebes, S., Schmidt-Kraepelin, M., & Sunyaev, A. (2020). IEEE Access, 8, 57075-57095.

sources.title: Proof of Learning (PoLe): Empowering Machine Learning with Consensus Building on Blockchains
Decentralized and Collaborative AI on Blockchain
sources.description: Harris, J. D., & Waggoner, B. (2019). IEEE Blockchain 2019, pp. 368-375.

sources.description: Harris, J. D. (2020). International Conference on Blockchain, pp. 142-153. Springer, Cham.

sources.description: Mittal, A., & Aggarwal, S. (2020). Frontiers in Blockchain, 3, 23.

sources.title: Proof of Federated Learning: A Novel Energy-Recycling Consensus Algorithm
sources.description: Qu, X., Wang, S., Hu, Q., & Cheng, X. (2021). IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems, 32(8), 2074-2085.

sources.title: A Mining Pool Solution for Novel Proof-of-Neural-Architecture Consensus
sources.description: Li, B., Lu, Q., Jiang, W., Jung, T., & Shi, Y. (2021). IEEE ICBC 2021, pp. 1-3.

sources.description: Go web framework inspired by Express, built on top of fasthttp for high performance.

sources.title: Dgraph

sources.title: Babylon.js

sources.description: High-performance messaging system for cloud-native, pub-sub, and request-reply communication patterns.

sources.description: A cross-language development platform for in-memory columnar data with libraries for efficient analytics and data interchange.

sources.description: Cloud-native high-performance edge and service proxy designed for large microservice architectures.

sources.description: Instant realtime GraphQL API layer generated over new or existing databases with fine-grained access control.

sources.description: Free-to-win rhythm game client and editor supporting multiple gameplay modes.
